findings.evidence: `HomotopyWith.prop'` only checks `P` on intermediate maps (`mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/Homotopy/Basic.lean:384-389`), while the roadmap source says isotopy is an embedding `F : X × I → Y × I` with `F(x,t)=(f(x,t),t)` (`roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/GeometricTopology/references/bz-knot-foundations.md:31-34`).
findings.fix: Define/store the level-preserving map, e.g. `fun p : I × X => (p.1, F p)` or equivalent, and require `IsEmbedding` of that total map; derive slice embeddings as lemmas if needed.
findings.issue: `Isotopy` only requires each time-slice `I -> C(X,Y)` to be an `IsEmbedding`, not that the level-preserving total map `I × X -> I × Y` is an embedding.

findings.evidence: The structure has only `isHomeomorph_apply' : ∀ t, IsHomeomorph ...` (`TauCeti/Topology/Homotopy/Isotopy.lean:108-112`), while the cited definition requires a level-preserving isotopy `H : Y × I → Y × I`, `H(y,t)=(h_t(y),t)` (`roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/GeometricTopology/references/bz-knot-foundations.md:38-40`).
findings.fix: Require `IsHomeomorph` (or the appropriate level-preserving isotopy predicate) for the total map `fun p : I × Y => (p.1, Φ p)`/equivalent, with `h₀ = id`; slice homeomorphisms can be consequences or additional lemmas.
findings.issue: `AmbientIsotopy` requires every slice `Y -> Y` to be `IsHomeomorph`, but not that the level-preserving map `Y × I -> Y × I` is the ambient isotopy from the source definition.

summary: The new foundations formalize isotopy/ambient isotopy as pointwise-in-time embedding/homeomorphism families, but the cited roadmap source requires the level-preserving total map on the product to be an embedding/homeomorphism. This is a semantic weakening in full generality, so downstream knot-equivalence and ambient-isotopy statements would be built on the wrong relation.
